draft.introduction: The Transfiguration in Mark’s account: Jesus’ garments become dazzling white, Moses and Elijah appear, and a voice from the cloud declares “This is my beloved Son.” The event became a cornerstone of Orthodox theosis theology.

translation.text: And after six days Jesus took with him Peter and James and John, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. And he was transfigured before them, and his clothes became radiant, intensely white, as no one on earth could bleach them. And there appeared to them Elijah with Moses, and they were talking with Jesus. And a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice came out of the cloud, “This is my beloved Son; listen to him.”

translator_notes.note: The verb μετεμορφώθη (“was transfigured”) is cognate with μορφή (“form”). Gregory Palamas built his theology of theosis on this event, arguing that the light the disciples saw was the uncreated divine energies, not reflected or created light.
